message:
chore: sync beads issue tracker state for bd-oy2 Asupersync migration progress

- Update bd-oy2 (Phase 3: Migrate test suites to Asupersync determinism)
  with latest progress comment: deterministic lab-backed TuiChannel
  delivery-order test added, RCH worker timeout noted
- Sync .br_history snapshots

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -104,7 +104,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-new.7","title":"Implement GitHub issues/PR collector + correlation","description":"## Background
Repo status is more useful when correlated with issues/PRs and repo activity. This integrates GitHub metadata to show “what’s blocked” and “what’s waiting.”

## Goals
- Collect issues/PR counts, states, and labels per repo.
- Correlate with ru repo_id for dashboards and alerts.
- Cache results to avoid API rate limits.

## Implementation
### Integration
- Use `gh` CLI or GitHub REST API with token from config.
- Support org-wide + per-repo queries.
- Cache ETag/If‑Modified‑Since to reduce API calls.

### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE gh_repo_issue_pr_snapshot(
    repo_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    open_issues INTEGER,
    open_prs INTEGER,
    triage_json TEXT,
    label_breakdown_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, collected_at)
);
```

## Risks
- API throttling; must respect rate limits.
- Repo mapping inconsistencies (path vs URL).

## Success Criteria
- Repo dashboard shows issue/PR counts and top labels.
- Collector respects GitHub rate limits.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for mapping repo_id to GH repo.
- Mock API tests for rate-limit handling.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/collectors/test_github_collect.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:09:46.345530803Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:09:46.345400507Z","close_reason":"GhCollector implemented and registered - github.rs with issue/PR collection, triage summary, label breakdown","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:21.363975126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:22.375529078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-33h.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.7","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:27.672542286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.8","title":"Implement cost attribution analytics","description":"## Background
We track usage (caut/caam) and session volume (cass) but lack cost attribution per repo/agent. This is crucial for optimizing spend and planning.

## Goals
- Attribute cost and usage to repo, machine, and agent type.
- Estimate cost per completed bead/issue where possible.
- Surface top cost drivers in TUI/web/robot.
- Emit confidence scores for attribution quality.

## Implementation
### Inputs
- caut usage snapshots, caam profiles, cass session stats, ru repo mappings.
- Map sessions to repo via cass metadata + repo paths.
- Confidence score derived from match quality (path match, session metadata, repo activity window).

### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE cost_attribution_snapshot(
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    repo_id TEXT,
    machine_id TEXT,
    agent_type TEXT,
    provider TEXT,
    estimated_cost_usd REAL,
    tokens_used BIGINT,
    sessions_count INTEGER,
    confidence REAL,
    raw_json TEXT
);
```

### Output
- “Top cost drivers” widget.
- `vc robot cost --json` summary.

## Risks
- Imperfect mapping of sessions to repos; document confidence score.

## Success Criteria
- Cost summaries match manual sanity checks within ±10%.
- Top 5 cost drivers visible in UI.
- Low-confidence rows are flagged.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for mapping + cost estimation.
- Fixture‑based tests for cross‑tool joins.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_cost_attribution.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T08:02:20.051359316Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T08:02:20.051263315Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:23.318314434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33h.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.8","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:38.588520035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.9","title":"Implement schema registry + robot/API docs generator","description":"## Background
Robot outputs and API schemas must remain stable for agents. A formal schema registry + doc generator prevents contract drift and makes integration safe.

## Goals
- Maintain JSON Schema definitions for robot outputs, config, and web API responses.
- Generate human‑readable docs + examples automatically.
- Validate outputs in tests (schema compliance).
- Detect schema changes in CI via diff checks.

## Implementation
### Schema Registry
- Store schema files under `docs/schemas/` with versioning.
- Add `vc robot-docs schemas|examples` to render docs.
- Include config schema for `vc.toml` validation.

### Validation
- Test helper to validate JSON against schema in unit tests and E2E.
- Fail fast if schema version mismatches.
- CI step to diff generated schemas vs committed ones.

## Risks
- Schema maintenance overhead; automate generation where possible.

## Success Criteria
- Robot outputs pass schema validation in CI.
- Docs list all schemas with examples.
- Schema diffs are explicit and reviewed.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for schema validation helpers.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_robot_schemas.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T16:34:52.036543109Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T16:34:52.036525606Z","close_reason":"Schema registry implemented: JSON schemas for robot outputs, validation helpers, E2E tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:24.352303753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3e2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.9","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:46.451400553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-oy2","title":"Phase 3: Migrate all test suites to Asupersync determinism","notes":"Background: All crates currently use #[tokio::test]. We must fully eliminate Tokio from the test environment to guarantee deterministic behavior.

Tasks:
1. Strip #[tokio::test] from all modules (vc_query, vc_store, vc_oracle, vc_cli, vc_web, etc.).
2. Replace them with Asupersync's test runner macro or manual asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new().block_on() blocks.
3. Utilize Asupersync's deterministic test labs (src/lab) to rewrite the most critical concurrent tests (e.g., the adaptive scheduler in vc_collect, channel operations in vc_alert), verifying mathematically that no deadlocks or race conditions exist.

Testing & Validation:
- Unit Tests: Ensure coverage remains at or above current levels. All 1,200+ tests must pass under the Asupersync runtime.
- E2E Tests: Implement e2e_determinism_check.sh to run the test suite multiple times under varying CPU loads to ensure test flakes are eradicated.
- Logging: Enhance test output logging so that if an Asupersync deterministic lab test fails, it prints the exact thread interleaving and scheduling decisions that led to the panic/deadlock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:50.465965811Z","created_by":"unknown","updated_at":"2026-03-10T05:32:37.006063410Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T05:32:37.005416970Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-125","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:51.524040490Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-1a4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:51.193664177Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:50.842597864Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-34e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:01:26.408117293Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-35x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:51.872400460Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-oy2","author":"codex","text":"Progress update: removed all remaining #[tokio::test] markers across the workspace and converted the affected vc_collect/vc_web tests to Asupersync-rooted harnesses. Remote verification is in progress via rch; the first workspace-wide cargo check was killed by the remote worker with exit 137 before surfacing any repo-local compile error, so I am retrying with reduced build parallelism.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:28:39Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-oy2","title":"Phase 3: Migrate all test suites to Asupersync determinism","notes":"Background: All crates currently use #[tokio::test]. We must fully eliminate Tokio from the test environment to guarantee deterministic behavior.

Tasks:
1. Strip #[tokio::test] from all modules (vc_query, vc_store, vc_oracle, vc_cli, vc_web, etc.).
2. Replace them with Asupersync's test runner macro or manual asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new().block_on() blocks.
3. Utilize Asupersync's deterministic test labs (src/lab) to rewrite the most critical concurrent tests (e.g., the adaptive scheduler in vc_collect, channel operations in vc_alert), verifying mathematically that no deadlocks or race conditions exist.

Testing & Validation:
- Unit Tests: Ensure coverage remains at or above current levels. All 1,200+ tests must pass under the Asupersync runtime.
- E2E Tests: Implement e2e_determinism_check.sh to run the test suite multiple times under varying CPU loads to ensure test flakes are eradicated.
- Logging: Enhance test output logging so that if an Asupersync deterministic lab test fails, it prints the exact thread interleaving and scheduling decisions that led to the panic/deadlock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:50.465965811Z","created_by":"unknown","updated_at":"2026-03-10T05:42:27.914844636Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T05:32:37.005416970Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-125","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:51.524040490Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-1a4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:51.193664177Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:50.842597864Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-34e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:01:26.408117293Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-oy2","depends_on_id":"bd-35x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:50:51.872400460Z","created_by":"unknown","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-oy2","author":"codex","text":"Progress update: removed all remaining #[tokio::test] markers across the workspace and converted the affected vc_collect/vc_web tests to Asupersync-rooted harnesses. Remote verification is in progress via rch; the first workspace-wide cargo check was killed by the remote worker with exit 137 before surfacing any repo-local compile error, so I am retrying with reduced build parallelism.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:28:39Z"},{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-oy2","author":"codex","text":"Progress update: added a deterministic lab-backed TuiChannel delivery-order test in vc_alert. Verification remains constrained by RCH worker policy rather than a surfaced Rust error: the narrow vc_alert check with a project-local target dir progressed further than before, but it was still terminated at roughly the five-minute remote timeout boundary.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:42:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-rf7","title":"Implement bv + br (Beads) collector","description":"Implement collector for bv (Beads Viewer) and br (Beads Rust) - task tracking and productivity metrics.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- bv --robot-triage for mega-command JSON output
- br ready --json for actionable work
- br sync --flush-only to export JSONL, then read .beads/issues.jsonl
- Use CLI Snapshot pattern

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE beads_triage_snapshots(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    repo_id TEXT,
    quick_ref_json TEXT,
    recommendations_json TEXT,
    project_health_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, repo_id)
);

CREATE TABLE beads_issues(
    repo_id TEXT,
    issue_id TEXT,
    status TEXT,
    priority TEXT,
    type TEXT,
    title TEXT,
    labels_json TEXT,
    deps_json TEXT,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, issue_id)
);

CREATE TABLE beads_graph_metrics(
    repo_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    pagerank_json TEXT,
    betweenness_json TEXT,
    critical_path_json TEXT,
    cycles_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, collected_at)
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement BeadsCollector with Collector trait
- Run bv --robot-triage in each repo with .beads directory
- Parse triage output for quick_ref, recommendations, health
- Store graph metrics (pagerank, betweenness, critical path)
- Handle missing bv/br binaries gracefully

REPO DISCOVERY:
- Get repo list from ru collector data
- Check each repo for .beads directory
- Only collect from repos with beads initialized

UI VALUE:
- What to work on next across all projects
- Project health at a glance
- Bottleneck identification
- Dependency visualization

TESTS:
- Create test .beads databases
- Mock bv --robot-triage output
- Verify issue synchronization

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.

 {"id":"bd-t32","title":"Re-establish workspace quality-gate verification via rch offload-only","description":"Follow-up after bd-27y: run cargo check/clippy/fmt validation in a way that stays offloaded through rch (avoid fail-open local execution), likely by crate-scoped runs and incremental triage of remaining warnings/errors.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:35:35.710271129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T17:40:42.418881729Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T17:40:42.418859878Z","close_reason":"Completed: identified vc_query as next clippy blocker and opened bd-2bz","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-tg1","title":"Implement TUI Guardian screen","description":"Implement the Guardian TUI screen showing healing protocol status, active interventions, and history.

SCREEN CONTENT:
- Guardian status: Active/Disabled, mode (suggest/execute)
- Active protocols: Currently running healing actions
- Pending interventions: Queued actions awaiting execution
- History: Recent healing actions and outcomes

LAYOUT:
+------------------------------------------------+
| GUARDIAN - SELF-HEALING            [t]oggle mode |
+------------------------------------------------+
| STATUS                                          |
| ├─ Mode: execute-safe (allowlisted commands)    |
| ├─ Patterns detected: 3 active                  |
| ├─ Last action: 2 min ago                       |
| └─ Success rate: 94% (47/50 last week)         |
+------------------------------------------------+
| ACTIVE PROTOCOLS                                |
| ├─ rate_limit_recovery on orko                  |
| │   └─ Step 2/4: Preparing account swap         |
| │   └─ Started: 45 sec ago                      |
| └─ disk_space_recovery on mac-mini              |
|     └─ Step 1/4: Analyzing disk consumers       |
+------------------------------------------------+
| PENDING INTERVENTIONS                           |
| ├─ cc_5 stuck_agent_recovery                    |
| │   └─ Waiting for: manual approval             |
| │   └─ Actions: send interrupt, force compact   |
+------------------------------------------------+
| HISTORY (last 24h)                              |
| ├─ [OK] rate_limit_recovery (orko) - 2min ago  |
| │   └─ Swapped jeff@email.com -> backup@email   |
| ├─ [OK] disk_cleanup (sydneymc) - 1hr ago      |
| │   └─ Cleared 12GB of agent caches             |
| ├─ [FAIL] stuck_agent (orko) - 3hr ago         |
| │   └─ Agent did not recover, escalated         |
+------------------------------------------------+

NAVIGATION:
- Tab between sections
- Enter on protocol to see details
- a to approve pending intervention
- r to reject/skip intervention
- p to pause guardian temporarily
- h to view full history

DATA SOURCES:
- Guardian state from vc_guardian crate
- Healing protocol definitions
- Audit events for history
- Alert correlation data

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Create guardian.rs in vc_tui/src/screens/
- Real-time protocol step progress
- Color-code by outcome (green=success, red=fail, yellow=pending)
- Show detailed audit trail for each action
- Support mode switching (off/suggest/execute-safe/with-approval)

TESTS:
- Snapshot tests for rendering
- Protocol state display tests
- History pagination tests

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/tui/test_guardian_screen.sh` with expect/pexpect.
- Capture logs and screenshots on failure; emit JSON summary in tests/logs/.
